commands.review_summary:
The PR guards approval-time reuse of existing same-key untagged device tokens and switches explicit loopback token/password CLI RPCs to backend gateway-client auth with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +343. Total +475 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main mints a new token for every approval, while the PR adds tests for stable same-key metadata refresh and unsafe reuse boundaries. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft changes auth-token semantics and local CLI gateway auth, so the next action is maintainer validation rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: fix(pairing): preserve stable device tokens on metadata refresh
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve existing untagged device tokens for same-key metadata refresh approvals when the token was already valid before the approval
- keep rotating for scope expansion, public-key repair, stale baseline tokens, revoked/issuer-tagged tokens, incompatible role/scopes, and malformed persisted token entries
- add pairing token regression coverage for Windows node reconnect churn and unsafe reuse boundaries
- make devices/nodes/gateway CLI RPC helpers use the direct local gateway-client admin path for explicit loopback token/password auth, avoiding stale paired CLI identity/scope traps

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts`: 60 passed
- `pnpm test:serial src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts src/cli/nodes-cli/register.invoke.approval-transport-timeout.test.ts src/cli/gateway-rpc.runtime.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts`: 90 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed`: passed core/coreTests lanes in Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksvhbjcvsmhysc6n5762khv3` / run `26674174999`
- Azure pond: signed Windows prerelease launches; operator pairing approved; node role upgrade approved; Windows node registered and reported `Nodes 1/1 online`

## Notes
Draft because exec-approval invocation validation against the signed Windows prerelease is still in progress.
target.author: vincentkoc
